title: Alert
description: Alert schema from FlashArray REST API

allOf.description:
A built-in resource. Many are singletons predefined by Purity (e.g., support
settings). Some correspond to a piece of software, like an app, or hardware,
like a controller. Others are created by the system in response to some event
(e.g., alerts, audit records).

Typically, a user can't create, delete or rename a built-in resource. A few
can be created or deleted, but not renamed because the names are meaningful
to Purity (e.g., VIFs).

id.description:
A globally unique, system-generated ID.
The ID cannot be modified and cannot refer to another resource.

name.description: A locally unique, system-generated name. The name cannot be modified.

context.description:
The context in which the operation was performed.

Valid values include a reference to any array which is a member of the same fleet
or to the fleet itself.

Other parameters provided with the request, such as names of volumes or snapshots,
are resolved relative to the provided `context`.
context.title: FixedReferenceWithType

allOf.description: Alert generated by the array when an event occurs.

actual.description: Actual condition at the time the alert is created.

category.description:
The category of the alert.
Valid values include `array`, `hardware` and `software`.

closed.description: The time the alert was closed in milliseconds since the UNIX epoch.

code.description: The code number of the alert.

component_name.description: The name of the component that generated the alert.

component_type.description: The type of component that generated the alert.

created.description: The time the alert was created in milliseconds since the UNIX epoch.

description.description: A short description of the alert.

expected.description: Expected state or threshold under normal conditions.

flagged.description:
If set to `true`, the message is flagged.
Important messages can can be flagged and listed separately.

knowledge_base_url.description: The URL of the relevant knowledge base page.

notified.description: The time the most recent alert notification was sent in milliseconds since the UNIX epoch.

severity.description:
The severity level of the alert.
Valid values include `info`, `warning`, `critical`, and `hidden`.

state.description:
The current state of the alert.
Valid values include `open`, `closing`, and `closed`.

updated.description: The time the alert was last updated in milliseconds since the UNIX epoch.
